metadata.method: hand-drafted, 4-pillar lens, vocabulary verified to EXIST in the corpus before inclusion
notes: Grounding killed most of the vocabulary a cold draft would have used: 'Pacto Histórico' 0 hits, 'Centro Democrático' 0, 'Registraduría' 0, 'Casa de Nariño' 0, 'CNE' 0, 'JEP' 0. Colombian election headlines in this corpus use CANDIDATE NAMES, not party or institution names.
Measured and kept: Espriella 103, Cepeda 35, Uribe 5, Quintero 1, Fajardo 1.

DROPPED bare election generics ('election', 'elecc', 'candidat'): 136 Colombian hits but 33 non-Colombian ANDEAN hits (~20% leak to Ecuadorian/Bolivian elections), and the candidate surnames already carry the story. Kept only the Colombia-specific electoral process terms (segunda vuelta / balotaje), which do not leak.
Candidate surnames are own-side Pillar 1 actors, not third-party leaders -- rule 4 excludes foreign leaders covering the FN, not the FN's own political class.
'Petro' deliberately excluded here too -- see colombia_us_alignment notes. Only 21 of his 184 hits are election-related.
'El Tigre' (the winner's nickname) omitted: 'Espriella' already covers those headlines and 'Tigre' is a collision risk.

AUDIT PRUNE: dropped 'Quintero' (1 hit, 100% foreign -- a different Quintero in AFRICA-EAST) and 'president-elect' (3 hits, 33% foreign to Israel/USA; Spanish 'presidente electo' covers the same ground at 0%). KEPT Espriella (103, 8% foreign) and Cepeda (35, 9%): that 'foreign' is AMERICAS-USA co-mention of the Trump endorsement -- benign co-occurrence, the Bardella case, not leak.

bundle.term: Colombian political principals of the transition
